rationale: The cited paper is used to motivate a core construct in the citing article: FEI is explicitly presented as being inspired by prior advertising-elasticity work, including Danaher and Dagger (2013). Later, the citing paper again uses Danaher and Dagger to frame the managerial logic that elasticity metrics support cost-effectiveness decisions. This is more than a background list mention because it helps justify the paper's conceptual setup and interpretation of its contribution, even though the specific empirical model is not reused.
relation_description: The citing paper applies the elasticity-based reasoning from traditional advertising research (as represented by Danaher and Dagger 2013) to influencer marketing by defining and using follower elasticity of impressions. It transfers the cited paper's managerial elasticity logic to a new domain rather than directly adopting its exact estimation design.
